sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.23 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: a) A subgroup$H$of a group$G$is called quasisubinvariant\/ if there is a normal system of$G$passing through$H$. Let${ K}$be a class of groups that is closed with respect to taking homomorphic images. A group$G$is called an$R^0({ K)$-group\/} if each of its non-trivial homomorphic images has a non-trivial quasisubinvariant${ K}$-sub\-group. Do$R^0({ K})$and$\,$coincide when${ K}$is the class of all...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
